claim: Neural networks find patterns in data that humans miss; interpretability methods can extract these patterns as actionable scientific discoveries
actionable: Train neural networks on scientific datasets and use interpretability to extract novel patterns humans wouldn't find through hypothesis-driven analysis
source_title: Automating Scientific Discovery - Jessica Rumbelow, Leap Labs

claim: Discovery Engine systematically extracts patterns via neural networks, contextualizes them with literature, and ranks by novelty rather than relying on hypothesis-driven research
actionable: Use systematic pattern extraction via neural networks instead of hypothesis-driven data analysis for faster, more unbiased scientific discovery
source_title: Automating Scientific Discovery - Jessica Rumbelow, Leap Labs

claim: Language models are fundamentally unsuited for numerical data analysis and instead serve better as synthesis tools integrated with data-driven discovery engines
actionable: Don't rely solely on LLMs for scientific data analysis; integrate them with specialized data-driven discovery tools to avoid hallucinations on numerical data
source_title: Automating Scientific Discovery - Jessica Rumbelow, Leap Labs

claim: Claude Opus in thinking mode hallucinated and overgeneralized on catalyst efficiency data, but combined with Discovery Engine found valid patterns
actionable: Evaluate LLM outputs on scientific data for hallucinations and pair LLMs with verified discovery tools rather than using LLMs standalone
source_title: Automating Scientific Discovery - Jessica Rumbelow, Leap Labs

claim: Synergistic patterns (combinatorial effects where multiple conditions together exceed individual effects) are computationally harder for humans to find than LLMs
actionable: Use neural networks to find synergistic patterns where combinations of conditions have disproportionate effects
source_title: Automating Scientific Discovery - Jessica Rumbelow, Leap Labs

claim: Discovery Engine is 100x faster than manual analysis by replacing iterative hypothesis-driven analysis with systematic pattern extraction over hours instead of months
actionable: For data-heavy scientific discovery, use automated pattern extraction to reduce months of manual analysis to hours
source_title: Automating Scientific Discovery - Jessica Rumbelow, Leap Labs

claim: Hypothesis-driven data analysis biases researchers toward expected findings, missing discoveries outside their assumptions
actionable: Complement hypothesis-driven research with unbiased systematic data exploration to discover unexpected patterns
source_title: Automating Scientific Discovery - Jessica Rumbelow, Leap Labs

claim: ArXiv is already filling with plausible but unverified LLM-generated papers, undermining scientific reproducibility and trust
actionable: For scientific work, require tools that produce verifiable, reproducible results rather than relying solely on LLM-generated claims
source_title: Automating Scientific Discovery - Jessica Rumbelow, Leap Labs
